extraction.one_line_summary: EVENEPOEL Remco won from 14-rider breakaway going clear at race start, dominated final climb after letting others work, consolidating GC lead over KUSS Sepp
tactical_patterns: EVENEPOEL sat on wheels entire breakaway until final climb, letting others work despite 8+ minute gap demonstrating polka dot priority.
KUSS team isolation forcing VINGEGAARD to work for him reverses typical Jumbo-Visma tactics from earlier mountain stages.
14-rider break with EVENEPOEL granted 8.5 minutes shows GC teams conceded stage with mountaintop finish to breakaway specialists.
MOSS Enric launched late sprint attack but EVENEPOEL wheel-sucked to line, proving better positioned sprinter than expected on steep finish.

rider_signals.EVENEPOEL Remco: positive: Tactical maturity letting breakaway work before attacking final climb, responding to Belgian press criticism with intelligent racing.
rider_signals.KUSS Sepp: positive: Successfully defended red jersey with VINGEGAARD working for him, critical time bonus gain over VINGEGAARD may secure overall by one second.
rider_signals.VINGEGAARD Jonas: neutral: Dropped on final climb but worked for KUSS in reversal of prior team hierarchy, gap to KUSS now 17 seconds.
rider_signals.BERNAL Egan: neutral: Early breakaway attempt shows improved power but didn't sustain effort into final 14-rider selection.
rider_signals.LANDA Mikel: neutral: Fourth place GC battle with AYUSO stable, position unchanged despite hard stage.
